engineGuide.h2: Nissan Wingroad Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Wingroad across all UK grey import generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK JDM specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The QG15DE replaced the GA15DE in 1999, bringing improved emissions and NEO technology variants - but also the timing chain stretch issue documented in Nissan TSB NTB09128.
compatibleVariants: Y11 1.5L Base, Y11 1.5L G, Y11 1.5L Aero, Y11 1.5L QG15DE-NEO, Y12 1.5L Base, Y12 1.5L G, Y12 1.5L CVT
commonFailures: Camshaft position sensor codes (P0340) often misdiagnosed
Secondary chain tensioner shoe wear causing rattle on startup
entries.cta: Get quotes for QG15DE Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: NEO variant of QG15DE with revised emissions calibration; shares identical timing chain architecture and failure patterns with standard QG15DE.
commonFailures: Chain tensioner failure more prevalent due to NEO calibration
P0340 cam sensor codes linked to chain stretch
EGR valve clogging on high-mileage urban-use imports
entries.cta: Get quotes for QG15DE-NEO Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The QG18DE offered increased torque and AWD compatibility for Y11/Y12 Wingroads, but retained the QG series timing chain vulnerability requiring NTB09128-compliant rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: Y11 1.8L Base, Y11 1.8L G, Y11 1.8L Aero, Y11 1.8L AWD, Y12 1.8L Base, Y12 1.8L G, Y12 1.8L CVT, Y12 1.8L AWD
commonFailures: AWD variants: oil pan and transfer case mount compatibility issues
Cam/crank correlation errors at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for QG18DE Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol AWD
entries.history: AWD-specific variant of QG18DE with reinforced block mounts and unique oil pan; timing chain failure patterns identical to standard QG18DE.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch compounded by AWD drivetrain load
Transfer case mount wear affecting chain tension
P0340 codes more frequent due to added drivetrain stress
entries.cta: Get quotes for QG18DE-AWD Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The GA15DE powered the original Y10 Wingroad; while robust, it shares the chain-driven architecture that would later manifest timing stretch issues in QG successors.
compatibleVariants: Y10 1.5L Base, Y10 1.5L G, Y10 1.5L Aero
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch at higher mileage (pre-NTB09128 design)
Oil sludge buildup on neglected service intervals
Cam sensor codes linked to chain wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for GA15DE Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Neo VVL
entries.history: The SR20VE Neo VVL brought variable valve lift technology to the Wingroad, delivering 190hp but also accelerated timing chain wear due to high-revving operation and complex valvetrain.
compatibleVariants: Y11 2.0L Neo VVL Base, Y11 2.0L Neo VVL Aero
commonFailures: Accelerated cam chain wear documented by owners and rental fleets
VVL solenoid sensitivity requiring specialist calibration
Timing chain stretch leading to complete engine failure in severe cases
entries.cta: Get quotes for SR20VE Nissan Wingroad engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Wingroad Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Wingroad's QG, GA and SR series petrol engines are generally robust but share a critical weakness: timing chain stretch affecting all generations. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the problem groups below to identify your issue and compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why Nissan Wingroad QG/GA/SR Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Wingroad Y10 (1996-1998, GA15DE), Y11 (1999-2005, QG15DE/QG18DE/SR20VE), Y12 (2005-2016, QG15DE/QG18DE)
problems.rootCause: Secondary timing chain tensioner wear causes chain slack, leading to rattling noise, cam/crank correlation errors and potential valve damage if ignored. Nissan TSB NTB09128 confirms design issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic check, chain tension measurement, sensor code scan
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable before catastrophic failure; identifies stretch early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace primary/secondary chains, tensioners, guides per NTB09128
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-70k miles if tensioners upgraded; does not address block wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with NTB09128-compliant upgraded components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles expected; warranty covers timing components; best value for high-mileage Wingroads

problems.recommendation: On a Wingroad valued £2,000-£5,000, a preventative chain inspection at 70k miles is cost-effective. If chain stretch is confirmed or P0340 codes appear, replacement with a rebuilt unit including upgraded tensioners offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt QG15DE engine prices for your Nissan Wingroad

problems.h4: Camshaft Position Sensor Misdiagnosis - P0340 Code Warning
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Wingroad Y11 (1999-2005, QG15DE/QG18DE/SR20VE), Y12 (2005-2016, QG15DE/QG18DE)
problems.rootCause: Stretched timing chain alters cam/crank timing correlation, triggering P0340/P0345 cam sensor codes. Mechanics often replace the sensor when the actual fault is chain stretch.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft position sensor, clear codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix only; chain stretch remains; codes likely to return

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates root cause; warranty covers timing system; recommended if sensor codes persist

problems.recommendation: Never replace a cam sensor on a Wingroad without first checking timing chain tension. If P0340 codes return after sensor replacement, the chain is stretched - budget for a rebuilt engine with NTB09128-compliant parts.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Wingroad P0340 timing chain repair

problems.group: SR20VE Neo VVL Accelerated Chain Wear
problems.h4: SR20VE Neo VVL Accelerated Chain Wear - Performance Variant Risk
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Wingroad Y11 (1999-2005, SR20VE Neo VVL 2.0L)
problems.rootCause: Higher-revving Neo VVL system places greater stress on timing chain; documented cases of accelerated wear leading to complete engine failure. Replacement engines historically scarce.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chains, tensioners, VVL solenoids; specialist calibration required
repairOptions.longevity: May extend life 30k-40k miles; requires Neo VVL-specialist garage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt SR20VE with upgraded timing components, VVL system tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; warranty covers Neo VVL components; limited specialist availability

problems.recommendation: SR20VE owners should budget for rebuilt engine replacement rather than repair. The Neo VVL system's complexity makes intermediate fixes less reliable; a rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components offers peace of mind.
problems.cta: Enquire about SR20VE Neo VVL rebuilt engine availability

problems.group: Secondary Chain Noise & Rattling on Startup
problems.h4: Secondary Chain Noise & Rattling on Startup
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Wingroad Y11 (1999-2005, all QG/GA/SR engines), Y12 (2005-2016, QG15DE/QG18DE)
problems.rootCause: Secondary timing chain tensioner shoe wear allows chain slap at specific RPM ranges; NTB09128 provides repair procedure for secondary chain noise.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace secondary chain tensioner shoes per NTB09128
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; primary chain may still stretch; monitor for recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with full NTB09128-compliant timing system upgrade
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates all chain-related risks; warranty covers timing components; recommended for high-mileage units

problems.recommendation: If rattling occurs only on cold startup and disappears after warm-up, tensioner shoe replacement may suffice. If noise persists at operating temperature or is accompanied by P0340 codes, replacement with a rebuilt engine is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare Nissan Wingroad timing chain repair vs replacement costs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Wingroad
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain stretch is the #1 cause of Nissan Wingroad engine replacement, but with the right specialist support, your JDM import can stay on the road. EngineMarket connects you with UK JDM import specialists who understand Wingroad timing chain issues, NTB09128-compliant rebuilt engines, and grey import fitting requirements. Compare quotes for supply-only or full supply & fit packages, all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Wingroad Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Wingroad and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Wingroad Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Wingroad across UK grey imports is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Nissan Wingroad 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L petrol engine is the most common Wingroad powerplant in the UK, spanning Y10 (GA15DE) and Y11/Y12 (QG15DE) generations. It offers balanced fuel economy and reliability when timing chain maintenance is observed, making it the default choice for budget-conscious JDM import owners.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch at 70k-90k miles; P0340 cam sensor codes often misdiagnosed
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Wingroad 1.5L petrol engines

items.title: Nissan Wingroad 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L QG18DE offers increased torque and the option of AWD on Y11 models, appealing to owners seeking extra capability. It shares the QG series timing chain architecture, so NTB09128-compliant rebuilt units are strongly recommended for longevity.
commonFailurePoints: Secondary timing chain guide wear; AWD variants require matching transfer case mounts
items.cta: Compare Nissan Wingroad 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Wingroad 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The SR20VE Neo VVL 2.0L is the performance flagship of the Wingroad range, featuring variable valve lift control for 190hp output. Its high-revving nature accelerates timing chain wear, making specialist rebuilt units with upgraded components essential for enthusiasts.
commonFailurePoints: Accelerated cam chain wear; VVL solenoid sensitivity; limited replacement availability
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan Wingroad 2.0L petrol engine availability

items.title: Nissan Wingroad Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: All UK Nissan Wingroad imports are petrol-powered, spanning GA, QG and SR series engines across Y10, Y11 and Y12 generations.

families: QG15DE - most common replacement engine (Y11/Y12 1.5L), timing chain stretch risk per NTB09128

SR20VE - performance Neo VVL 2.0L (Y11), accelerated cam chain wear reported by owners

engineYears.h2: Nissan Wingroad Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Wingroad's production timeline (1996-2016), organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from JDM specialists.

years.description: The original Y10 Wingroad launched in 1996 based on the Nissan Sunny platform, exclusively with the GA15DE 1.5L petrol engine.
keyChanges: Y10 generation introduced; first Wingroad model
GA15DE 1.5L petrol engine only; no diesel option
Based on Nissan Sunny B14 chassis; front-wheel drive only
knownFor: Robust GA15DE engine with simple chain-driven design
Early examples now high-mileage; timing chain inspection essential
Rare in UK; mostly enthusiast-owned grey imports
Affordable replacement engines but verify chain history
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-1998 Nissan Wingroad replacement engines
years.ticker: Y10 Wingroad engine replacement • GA15DE timing chain check • Nissan Wingroad 1996 engine cost

years.description: The Y11 generation launched in 1999 with the new QG15DE and QG18DE engines, replacing the GA series and introducing NEO technology variants.
keyChanges: Y11 generation introduced; new platform based on Nissan Sunny B15
NEO emissions technology variant available on QG15DE
AWD option introduced on QG18DE models
knownFor: QG series timing chain stretch issues first reported on high-mileage units
NEO variants show slightly higher tensioner failure rates
AWD models require matching transfer case configuration
Most common UK import generation; highest replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2000 Nissan Wingroad replacement engines
years.ticker: Y11 Wingroad QG15DE engine • Nissan Wingroad timing chain problems • Wingroad 1.8L AWD engine replacement

years.preview: Y11 Facelift: SR20VE Neo VVL Performance Variant
years.description: The 2001 facelift introduced the high-performance SR20VE Neo VVL 2.0L engine, targeting enthusiasts but bringing accelerated timing chain wear concerns.
keyChanges: Variable valve lift control technology added
Aero trim expanded across engine range
Minor exterior refresh; mechanicals unchanged for QG/GA engines
mainEngines: SR20VE - 2.0L petrol Neo VVL (Y11 performance variant)
popularModels: Nissan Wingroad Y11 2.0L Neo VVL Aero
knownFor: SR20VE Neo VVL documented accelerated cam chain wear by owners
QG15DE-NEO shows higher tensioner failure rates than standard QG15DE
AWD variants require specialist fitting due to unique mounts
Peak UK import years; highest volume of replacement enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Nissan Wingroad replacement engines
years.ticker: SR20VE Neo VVL engine replacement • Wingroad P0340 cam sensor code • Y11 Wingroad rebuilt engine cost

years.preview: Y12 Generation: Tiida Platform & CVT Option
years.description: The Y12 generation moved to the Nissan Tiida platform in 2005, retaining QG series engines but adding CVT transmission options and updated emissions systems.
keyChanges: Y12 generation introduced; based on Nissan Tiida C11 platform
QG15DE and QG18DE engines carried over with minor updates
Updated emissions calibration for Euro 4/5 compliance
knownFor: CVT variants require ECU adaptation during engine replacement
Timing chain stretch issues persist; NTB09128 still applicable
Later examples (2010+) show improved chain tensioner durability
Lower UK import volume than Y11; specialist sourcing required
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2016 Nissan Wingroad replacement engines
years.ticker: Y12 Wingroad CVT engine • Nissan Wingroad 2005 engine replacement • QG18DE AWD fitting cost

faq.h2: Nissan Wingroad Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Nissan Wingroad engine in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, for clean JDM imports valued £2,000-£5,000. Rebuilt engines from £1,600 with 12-month warranty offer better value than scrapping a well-maintained Wingroad with good bodywork and low chassis mileage.
keyPoints: Wingroad JDM imports retain enthusiast value in the UK market
Rebuilt QG15DE engines cost £1,600-£2,000 vs vehicle value £2k-£5k
Timing chain-upgraded rebuilt units prevent repeat failures
Supply & fit packages from JDM specialists simplify the process
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Nissan Wingroad engine prices for your variant

items.question: How much does a Nissan Wingroad engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Used engines: £600-£1,800 (high timing chain risk). Rebuilt engines: £1,500-£2,800 (NTB09128-compliant upgraded parts). Supply & fit labour: £300-£600 additional.
keyPoints: QG15DE rebuilt: £1,600-£2,000 supply only
SR20VE Neo VVL rebuilt: £2,200-£2,800 (specialist premium)
Labour for fitting: £300-£600 from JDM import specialists
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get a tailored Nissan Wingroad engine replacement quote

items.question: Does the Nissan Wingroad have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: ALL Nissan Wingroad petrol engines (GA15DE, QG15DE, QG18DE, SR20VE) use timing CHAINS, not belts. No routine replacement interval, but inspect at 70k-90k miles for stretch.
keyPoints: Timing chains require no scheduled replacement like belts
Stretch occurs at 70k-90k miles; causes rattling and P0340 codes
Nissan TSB NTB09128 addresses secondary chain noise/failure
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioners per NTB09128
items.cta: Check Nissan Wingroad timing chain inspection costs

items.question: What does fault code P0340 mean on a Nissan Wingroad?
items.answer: P0340 indicates Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit. On Wingroads, this is often MISDIAGNOSED - the actual fault is usually a stretched timing chain, not the sensor itself.
keyPoints: P0340 triggered by cam/crank timing correlation error
Stretched chain alters timing; sensor reads incorrectly
Replacing sensor alone rarely fixes the root cause
Always check chain tension before replacing cam sensor
items.cta: Compare Nissan Wingroad P0340 timing chain repair quotes

items.question: What are the most common Nissan Wingroad engine problems?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch (NTB09128) is the #1 issue across all QG/GA/SR engines. Secondary chain noise, cam sensor misdiagnosis (P0340), and SR20VE accelerated wear also feature prominently.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch affects all Wingroad petrol engines
P0340 codes often misdiagnosed; check chain first
SR20VE Neo VVL shows accelerated cam chain wear
Rebuilt engines with NTB09128 parts prevent repeat failures
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Wingroad timing chain replacement

items.question: Where can I buy a Nissan Wingroad engine in the UK?
items.answer: Compare quotes from UK JDM import specialists via EngineMarket. Used, rebuilt and reconditioned QG15DE/QG18DE/SR20VE engines available with supply-only or full supply & fit options nationwide.
keyPoints: Wingroad never sold in UK; all parts via JDM import channels
EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK JDM specialists
Rebuilt units include NTB09128-compliant upgraded parts
Free comparison tool matches your registration to compatible engines
items.cta: Find Nissan Wingroad engine suppliers near you

items.question: How long does a Nissan Wingroad engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery: 1-3 days for QG15DE/QG18DE. Full supply & fit: 1-2 days for manual variants, 2-3 days for CVT/AWD requiring ECU adaptation.
keyPoints: QG15DE/QG18DE engines commonly in UK stock
CVT and AWD variants require additional coding time
JDM specialists familiar with grey import fitting procedures
Road test and warranty documentation included on completion
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan Wingroad engine supply & fit turnaround

items.question: Do rebuilt Nissan Wingroad engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes. All rebuilt engines supplied via EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines typically carry 3-6 months; confirm timing chain coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt warranty covers timing components per NTB09128
12-month unlimited mileage standard on rebuilt units
Used engine warranties shorter; verify chain inspection history
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier pre-purchase
items.cta: Compare Nissan Wingroad engine warranty terms

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code to my Nissan Wingroad?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but complex. QG15DE/QG18DE share architecture; SR20VE requires VVL-specialist fitting. Always verify ECU, wiring harness and transmission compatibility first.
keyPoints: QG15DE and QG18DE share mounting points; easier swap
SR20VE Neo VVL requires VVL-calibrated ECU and harness
AWD variants need matching transfer case and oil pan
JDM specialists can advise on compatible swap options
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan Wingroad engine compatibility checks

items.question: How do I identify my Nissan Wingroad engine code?
items.answer: The engine code stamp is located on the block, driver side, near the timing chain cover. Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified identification of your exact engine code and variant.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near timing cover (driver side)
QG15DE, QG18DE, GA15DE, SR20VE are the four main codes
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact specification
EngineMarket matches your code to compatible rebuilt units
items.cta: Identify your Nissan Wingroad engine code instantly
